assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSOR 1 — real_world_framing_006 (Run 3)

The retrospective comparison confounds selection effects with model quality — the 3.1% vs. 3.4% default difference reflects different population compositions, not a controlled model quality test. Both expansion regimes are out of distribution: the training data excludes FICO <660 and loans >$25K by design. No prospective shadow testing is planned. The expansion requires a validation phase before live deployment in either expanded regime.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSOR 2 — real_world_framing_006 (Run 3)

Three issues: (1) retrospective comparison is selection-confounded; (2) OOD expansion — both targets excluded from training; (3) no shadow validation plan. The model's in-distribution performance is strong but does not predict out-of-distribution performance. Prospective shadow testing of both expansion populations is required before live authority expansion.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSOR 3 — real_world_framing_006 (Run 3)

The proposal presents a retrospecively-confounded comparison as evidence for expansion into unvalidated regimes, without shadow testing. All three methodological problems are present. Prospective shadow validation of both expanded populations for 6 months is the minimum required before any live expansion.
synthesis_raw:
SYNTHESIS — real_world_framing_006 (Ensemble, Run 3)

Consensus across all three assessors: selection confound, OOD expansion, no shadow testing. Unanimous recommendation for prospective shadow validation before live expansion.

EMPIRICAL TEST SPECIFICATION: Shadow-mode prospective underwriting of both expansion populations for 6 months with human review making binding decisions. Expansion proceeds only if shadow performance meets pre-specified calibration and default rate benchmarks.

empirical_test.condition: 6-month shadow underwriting of FICO 620–659 and $25,001–$50,000 populations with human review.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Shadow default rates materially exceed current-scope benchmark.
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Shadow performance within 0.5 pp for both populations.
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Asymmetric generalization across the two expansion dimensions.
